title: New system tests under BATS
body:
Initial attempt at writing a framework for podman system tests.
The idea is to define a useful set of primitives that will
make it easy to write actual tests and to interpret results
of failing ones.

This is a proof-of-concept right now; only a small number of
tests, by no means comprehensive. I am requesting review in
order to find showstopper problems: reasons why this approach
cannot work. Should there be none, we can work toward running
these as gating tests for Fedora and RHEL8.

Signed-off-by: Ed Santiago <santiago@redhat.com>

comments.body: Oh fantastic, perfect timing, I just got https://github.com/containers/libpod/pull/2435 to pass CI also.  I'll take a peek at this first thing Tuesday.

@edsantiago this is great, more than good enough starting point.  I didn't actually run anything yet because...let me get `jq` installed (anything else?) on all our CI VM images.  That will support running the tests across all our platforms.  Again, that's not intended for official use, it's just a short-cut to support the pull-request and review workflow...
@cevich thank you! For your helpful feedback and kind words. What would you think of making the json tests skippable in the absence of `jq`?

Given the amount of JSON involved, no, I think it's a basic-requirement we need to have as a dependency.  It's not hard to add to the VM images, the hard part is waiting for the associated PR to merge, then running a few commands.  Anything else you can think of that might be handy tooling wise?

(Look in `./contrib/cirrus/packer/*_setup.sh` for what we have now)

I'm fairly sure there are no other requirements yet. It's possible that future tests could use `netcat` but I'm not even close to there yet -- that may be too hairy for BATS testing. (I'll look into it anyway)
Yeah, we def. want system-tests to be high-level, light and fluffy.  In fact, I nearly forgot I had that other PR open...should we just merge #2257 document into here?
Ooh, thanks for the reminder. #2257 is a great reference - why don't you merge it, and I'll rebase my PR and mark off progress on `TODO.md` ?
Force push: rebase, implement review feedback, many other fixes
Having heard no objection at demo time, I'd like to submit this for merge. That will let me start working on packaging it for Fedora and RHEL, while continuing to develop further tests.

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *edsantiago*, *mheon* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [mheon] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
Tests failing becaus quay.io is down; known outage; no word on ETA.
